header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Relocation — SF or NYC)

summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Program Operations Manager who thrives on building systems that keep distributed programs running, tracking data for reliable decision-making, and coordinating across functions to scale with precision. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve operational experience—through human-centered, data-driven approaches.

bullets.text: Upskill HR team in {data|data analytics} to audit 300+ leave cases, successfully refuting cost assumptions via {data|evidence-based insights}
Empower HR to present {data|executive-ready insights} on $362K in leave costs, leading to policy redesigns and first aid training
Manage {prog|20+ programs} and {prog|10+ vendors}, maintaining {data|data accuracy} across tracking systems and refining {coord|operations} via feedback analysis
bullets.original: Manage 20+ programs and 10+ vendors, refining program positioning, delivery strategy, and operations via feedback analysis
bullets.reason: JD: 'data integrity — keep tracking systems accurate and current' + 'vendor management' — leads with data accuracy and vendor ops, the two JD anchors

bullets.text: Build {coord|onboarding documentation} and workflows for 70+ {cross|cross-functional} staff, reducing ramp-up time by 75%

bullets.text: Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming {data|feedback data} into actionable reports that informed engagement strategies
Analyzed 2,000+ employee experiences to amplify employee voice and guide C-suite decisions on satisfaction drivers

Elevated {ex|employee experience} via 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects focusing on survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D

bullets.original: Facilitated smooth organizational change by directing a Mergers & Acquisitions project, overseeing cross-functional stakeholders
bullets.reason: JD: 'access management — grant, audit, and maintain access across collaboration tools and internal systems' + 'data integrity' — data migration in M&A maps directly

bullets.original: Optimized onboarding processes across 3 global locations by conducting process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities

bullets.text: Initiated a DEI pilot with an Inclusive Leadership workshop for 12 senior leaders, later expanded to 23 other global locations
Strengthened engagement via an HR newsletter and award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ competitors
Developed proficiency across various HR functions, ranging from recruitment and compensation to performance management
Filled a challenging international role by drafting job descriptions, posting openings, and researching global compensation details
Engaged global talent via personalized communication and customer service, increasing applications by 566% in 2 months
Organized recruiting events and moderated panels with C-suite at top 3 universities, reaching 230+ students and 80+ applicants

bullets: Relevant Coursework: Organizational Psychology, Research Methods, Group Dynamics & Leadership, Consumer Psychology

education.degree: BA in Psychology and Education Studies (GPA: 4.00)

projects: Consulting Projects: Informed Elanco Animal Health employer branding & talent attraction strategy via survey design/execution
Master's Capstone: Examined non-monetary factors shaping employer attractiveness for YouTube via mixed-method research
Scholarships: Freeman Asian Scholarship (4-year full-tuition), Tuck Bridge Scholarship (full)

Certifications: HRCI Human Resource Associate Certificate (in progress)

items.reason: InGenius #1: 'Manage 20+ programs and 10+ vendors' — primary vendor mgmt proof point

items.reason: ODN #2: '$362K in leave costs — executive-ready insights' · NextGen: 'analyzed 2,000+ employee responses, reporting insights to C-suite'

items.text: {cross|Cross-Functional Collaboration:} Work with research teams, IT, and vendor partners; comfortable executing at high volume in a still-evolving program.
items.reason: InGenius #4: '25+ global stakeholders'; comfortable in ambiguous, high-volume operational contexts

items.text: 4+ years in program operations, research operations, recruiting coordination, or vendor management (Jamie: ~3 years, stretch)
Owned an operational process end-to-end with accountability for outcomes
Comfortable as primary point of contact for large, distributed population
Strong detail orientation and data accuracy focus
Experience using AI tools for drafting, data work, and automation
Preferred: program scaling experience, external workforce/vendor ops, light scripting
